title: At-least-once, at-most-once, exactly-once
description: Most systems deliver at-least-once, so consumers must be idempotent; true exactly-once requires transactional sinks or dedup keys.
body: Use when reasoning about duplicates and message processing. At-most-once can lose data on failure; at-least-once (the common default in Kafka and retried batch jobs) can deliver duplicates; exactly-once is achieved not by magic but by idempotent writes, dedup on an event key, or transactional offsets+sink. Do not assume a system gives exactly-once just because it advertises it. Actions: make consumers idempotent regardless, and add dedup keys for streams. Validation signals include stable counts under forced retries. Risk boundary: relying on a sink's exactly-once claim without idempotent logic invites silent duplication on edge cases.
